Why Charles River · Why this role

A program manager who happens to hold a PhD — not a scientist trying to pivot.

The hardest part of the Associate Director SPM role is not the project management tools. It is sitting between a Chief Scientific & Innovation Officer and a cross-functional team and making sure both sides are always oriented toward the same outcome. You need enough scientific depth to translate what the CSIO actually means. Enough operational fluency to convert that meaning into a timeline and a risk register. Enough communication discipline to ensure every stakeholder has exactly the right information at exactly the right moment.

That is the work I have been doing for seven years — without the title. At UCLA, I run the operating cadences, own the stakeholder briefings, and manage the risk escalation workflows for 6 concurrent clinical programs. At Duke, I led a 4-site international program with its own feasibility review process, data governance, and cross-institutional reporting structure. At LAUTECH, I built the entire SOPs, playbooks, and training infrastructure from scratch — and scaled a scientific operation from 30% to 100% capacity in 6 months through change management.

Charles River has built something rare: a CRO with both the breadth of a 110-facility global network and the scientific precision behind 80% of FDA approvals. The CSIO function at this scale is exactly where operational program management creates the most leverage — and where I want to do this work.

45%
Reduction in program turnaround time through structured operating cadences and milestone management at UCLA
50%
Reduction in compute costs through cloud migration change initiative — cross-institutional alignment across 4 international sites
3 yrs
Zero data quality breaches across 4 international sites — continuous audit readiness without a single gap
$162K

Case studies

Problems I solved. How I solved them. What it produced.

Three real-world program management challenges — each one directly mapping to the Associate Director SPM duties at Charles River.

01
Operating Rhythm & Stakeholder Alignment — UCLA (30% of JD)
UCLA David Geffen School of Medicine · Bioinformatics Operations Lead · Jul 2025 – Present
+

The Problem

Six concurrent clinical-stage translational programs were running simultaneously with no standardized operating rhythm. Decision bottlenecks were slowing program timelines. Status updates were fragmented across individual scientists with no unified reporting to senior scientific leadership. Risk events — flagged biospecimens, pipeline failures — lacked a formal escalation pathway.

What I Built

Designed and implemented a weekly operating cadence: standups, milestone reviews, and risk/issue scrubs. Built executive status briefings tailored for PI and cross-functional partners. Created a real-time escalation workflow routing flagged data events directly to principal investigators. Authored decision-ready change-control proposals for all program-level changes. Established a unified QC dashboard for specimen status, pipeline progress, and risk flags.

Outcomes

45%
Reduction in end-to-end program turnaround time
Zero
Chain-of-custody breaks across 1,200+ biospecimens
3 CDx
Companion diagnostic submissions advanced on schedule
02
Multi-Site Program Execution & Change Management — Duke (30% + 20% of JD)
Duke University, Dept. of Evolutionary Anthropology · Scientific Program Lead · Sep 2022 – Jun 2025
+

The Problem

A multi-institutional program spanning 4 international sites lacked standardized feasibility review, data quality assurance, and cross-institutional governance. Audit readiness was reactive. A major cloud migration was required to scale programs that had outgrown HPC infrastructure — needing change-management buy-in across geographically dispersed teams with different operational cultures.

What I Built

Formalized feasibility assessment process for all incoming protocols: capacity, timelines, resource documentation. Built QA tools and escalation training for junior staff. Drove cloud migration (AWS: EC2, S3, EMR, Batch, Spark) through change-management engagement across 4 institutions. Established FAIR-compliant data governance for real-time collaboration. Prepared and delivered NIH grant deliverables and cross-functional briefings to senior leadership.

Outcomes

Zero
Data quality breaches across 3 years of 4-site operations
50%
Reduction in compute costs through cloud migration change initiative
2 NIH
Grant deliverables completed on schedule
03
Standardization, SOPs & Change Management from Zero — LAUTECH (20% + 20% of JD)
Humboldt Research Hub, LAUTECH, Nigeria · Laboratory Manager & Coordinator · Jan 2022 – Aug 2022
+

The Problem

A multi-site scientific operation running at 30% capacity with no SOPs, no formal training infrastructure, no adverse event escalation pathway, and no standardized documentation across 10+ sites. There was no playbook for anything. Regulatory risk was high. The organization needed a complete operational build — equivalent to standing up a CRO site function from scratch.

What I Built

Authored comprehensive IRB-aligned SOPs for all procedures across all 10+ sites. Designed and delivered structured change-management training for 50+ personnel — informed consent, chain-of-custody, AE reporting, regulatory documentation. Standardized enrollment, visit, and documentation workflows. Built budget tracking, inventory management, and reagent procurement systems. Converted reactive compliance into continuous audit readiness.

Outcomes

30→100%
Operational capacity in 6 months
50+
Personnel trained and audit-competent
First
Standardized playbook in the organization's history
90-Day delivery plan

My first 90 days at Charles River

Specific, committed, and built around the CSIO's four core priorities from the job description.

Days 1–30

Listen, Map & Earn Trust

Shadow the CSIO and cross-functional partners to map existing operating rhythms, dashboards, and reporting tools.

  • Audit active program portfolios and risk registers for gaps and quick wins
  • Map stakeholder landscape and key decision-making moments in the annual calendar
  • Understand GLP/GCP program context and regulatory environment across Charles River's portfolio
  • Review existing cadences, templates, and SOPs — identify the first standardization opportunity
Days 31–60

Ship an Operating Rhythm

Stand up at least one new cadence that improves alignment or decision speed for the CSIO.

  • Launch a milestone review or risk/issue scrub that the CSIO immediately finds useful
  • Begin producing weekly status updates usable directly for stakeholder communication
  • Identify one SOP or reporting gap and deliver a first-version improvement
  • Build the first decision log and change-control template tailored to Charles River's programs
Days 61–90

Demonstrate Ownership

Manage at least one risk event or change-control proposal end-to-end. Deliver a program readout to senior leadership.

  • Present a playbook or template recommendation for one recurring process to the CSIO
  • Begin contributing to cross-functional planning for an upcoming program milestone
  • Attend at least one external meeting with the CSIO as operational support
  • Document one measurable improvement to program speed, clarity, or accountability

Skills

Scientific project management toolkit

Program Management & Execution

Planning · Milestones · Risk · Budgets
  • Milestone planning, dependency mapping, and resource allocation
  • Risk/issue registers — proactive surfacing and mitigation plans
  • Budget tracking and variance reporting to leadership
  • Operating cadences: standups, milestone reviews, risk scrubs
  • Feasibility assessment for incoming protocols and programs
  • Dashboard and KPI reporting for cross-functional stakeholders

Internal Communications & Stakeholder Alignment

Briefings · Reporting · Decision Logs
  • Executive briefings and status reports tailored by audience level
  • Decision logs and change-control proposals
  • Cross-functional stakeholder alignment across scientific and operational teams
  • Peer-reviewed publications (28+) and NIH grant deliverables
  • Conference presentations at SMBE, IPS Congress, ASP
  • Microsoft Office suite: Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Project

Standardization & Process Excellence

SOPs · Playbooks · RACI · GLP/GCP
  • SOP authorship from scratch — 10+ site deployment at LAUTECH
  • Playbook and template development for recurring processes
  • Training program design for 50+ personnel in regulatory compliance
  • GCP/IRB-aligned documentation and audit-readiness practice
  • GLP/GCP context in CRO and academic medical environments
  • Version-controlled documentation systems with chain-of-custody

Change Management & Science Depth

Change · Cloud · Genomics · Translational
  • Cloud migration change management — AWS across 4 international institutions
  • Workforce change management — 30%→100% capacity in 6 months
  • Computational genomics, NGS pipelines, multi-omics (scientific credibility)
  • Python, R, SQL, Nextflow, AWS, HPC — data-driven program reporting
  • Translational science: companion diagnostics, IND-enabling programs, oncology
  • Multi-site field operations across 4 continents and 10+ countries
